title: Phyllorkis cirrhata
taxonomy: Phyllorkis cirrhata is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Orchidoideae and the tribe Coelogyneae. The genus Phyllorkis is closely related to the genus Coelogyne. This classification is based on the plant's physical characteristics and morphological features. Further research is needed to confirm the exact classification of Phyllorkis cirrhata within the orchid family.

intro: Phyllorkis cirrhata, also known as the Coralroot, is a type of orchid native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that may aid in the treatment of various ailments, such as wounds and skin conditions.
